_comment: OpenCode adapter assembly spec. Tells gen/generate.py how to build the full
.opencode/ adapter (+ the root AGENTS.md) from the SAME neutral source as the
Claude adapter — no re-carving of any body. The generator stays THIN (arch
note 'How thin the metalanguage can be'): it inlines a neutral body, prepends
the OpenCode frontmatter from this manifest, and copies the config + entry
surface artifacts verbatim. No parsing, no re-serialization — so the output is
deterministic (same inputs -> byte-identical outputs).
Frontmatter shape per https://opencode.ai/docs/agents/ : `description` +
`mode: subagent` + a `tools` OBJECT map (NOT Claude's comma-list). Agent
files land under .opencode/agent/ (the filename is the agent id); command
files under .opencode/command/. The always-on core is wired via the
`instructions` array in opencode.json (https://opencode.ai/docs/config/), the
OpenCode analogue of the Claude @-import.
PREVIEW: the deny-list enforcement plugin and cross-model model pins are
intentionally NOT built here (later slices; the plugin is gated on Spike B).
Each section names the harness-neutral capability (see
src/manifests/capabilities.json) it realizes, so the same neutral bodies are
re-targeted to OpenCode without touching src/agents|commands/*.body.md.
The OpenCode CORE enforcement plugin. Emitted from a template
(opencode/plugin/<name>.js.tmpl) with the wb-* role deny-list injected by
the generator FROM the Claude settings.json Task|Agent|Skill matcher (the
single authored copy of that role set). The __WB_DENY_ROLES__ placeholder
in the template is replaced with the extracted role array — so there is
exactly one authored copy and the two adapters cannot drift. Per
https://opencode.ai/docs/plugins/ : tool.execute.before throws to deny.
Verified subagent-effective on OpenCode 1.16.2 (Spike B, 2026-06-07).
REGISTRATION: the file AUTO-LOADS from .opencode/plugin/ — confirmed on the
real loader (opencode debug config --pure lists it in the resolved plugin
set with NO opencode.json entry). An explicit `plugin` array entry would
DOUBLE-register the same file (auto-load + config), so opencode.json
deliberately carries no plugin entry.
